activeSlice.name: winui-music-loopback-wall-clock-padding
activeSlice.goal: preserve wall-clock capture duration in loopback WAV data and make materialization fail closed when raw WAV data does not cover the reported capture/CDB decode window
activeSlice.claimBoundary: helper/materializer/diagnostic hardening only; no BEA launch, no CDB attach, no byte patch, no Ghidra mutation, no game payload redistribution, no accepted audible-output proof, and no online proof.
latestFindings: capture_audio_loopback.py now inserts zero-silence padding for WASAPI loopback quiet gaps so WAV byte offsets preserve wall-clock capture duration.
capture_audio_loopback.py validates wallClockPadding metadata, byte-rate/block-align fields, and derived WAV data duration against requested capture duration.

winui_safe_copy_music_audible_output_materializer.py now derives raw WAV data duration from canonical RIFF sample-rate/block-align/data-frame math and rejects forged byteRate/blockAlign headers, non-frame-aligned data, missing/inconsistent wallClockPadding metadata, summaries whose timestamps outlast the raw WAV data span, and spans that miss the CDB decode window.
The old music-audible-live-20260624-144834 bundle remains rejected; runtimeAudibleOutputProof stays false until a new private raw bundle passes materializer plus final checker.

consultReview.specialist: Carson confirmed stopwatch/capture ordering, WAV header/duration guards, and false audible-proof boundary; accepted blocker was missing materializer enforcement for helper-authored wallClockPadding metadata.
consultReview.adversarial: Hilbert caught the forged byteRate/header-duration bypass; fixed with canonical blockAlign/byteRate/data alignment validation.
consultReview.docsAudit: Plato caught stale state overclaiming broad validation and stale capability/re_orchestrator wording; fixed in touched-lane docs/state.

musicDecodeWindowDiagnostic.nextAction: rerun a new private live bundle only after wall-clock padding hardening is committed; old rejected bundle remains rejected
